role_summary: Co-founder and captain of the Seattle chapter of the Black Panther Party, 1968-1972.
biographical_paragraph: The Seattle chapter of the Black Panther Party formed in 1968 with Aaron Dixon as its founding captain, the first such chapter to organize beyond California [src: 1]. Dixon was nineteen when he took the post, having been born in Chicago on January 2, 1949 [src: 1]. Under his captaincy through 1972 the Seattle chapter ran a Free Breakfast for Children program and opened a community health and legal services clinic still in operation today as the Carolyn Downs Family Medical Center [src: 1]. The same year the chapter formed, Dixon traveled to Oakland for Bobby Hutton's funeral and met Kathleen Cleaver there for the first time; Cleaver is also represented in this archive, at entry 73 [src: 1]. Four decades later, in 2012, Dixon returned to that organizing arc in book form with the Haymarket Books memoir titled My People Are Rising: Memoir of a Black Panther Party Captain [src: 1]. His Library of Congress oral history sits at entry 1 of the Civil Rights History Project [src: 2].

sources.title: Aaron Dixon oral history
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress
